2604.00997.title: Uncertainty-Aware Variational Reward Factorization via Probabilistic Preference Bases for LLM Personalization
2604.00997.paper_summary: Reward factorization personalizes large language models (LLMs) by decomposing rewards into shared basis functions and user-specific weights. Yet, existing methods estimate user weights from scarce data in isolation and as deterministic points, leading to inaccurate and unreliable inference. We introduce Variational Reward Factorization (VRF), an uncertainty-aware framework that represents each user's preferences as a variational distribution in a shared preference space. VRF infers user distributions via a variational encoder, derives weights through Wasserstein distance matching with shared probabilistic bases, and downweights uncertain estimates through a variance-attenuated loss. On three benchmarks, VRF outperforms all baselines across seen and unseen users, few-shot scenarios, and varying uncertainty levels, with gains extending to downstream alignment.
2604.00997.paper_summary_zh: 獎勵因子分解通過將獎勵分解為共享基函數和用戶特定權重來個性化大型語言模型（LLMs）。然而，現有方法從稀缺數據中孤立估計用戶權重，並作為確定性點，導致推斷不準確且不可靠。我們引入了變分獎勵因子分解（VRF），這是一個關注不確定性的框架，將每個用戶的偏好表示為共享偏好空間中的變分分佈。VRF 通過變分編碼器推斷用戶分佈，通過與共享概率基礎的 Wasserstein 距離匹配來推導權重，並通過方差衰減損失來降低不確定估計的權重。在三個基準測試中，VRF 在已見和未見用戶、少量樣本場景以及不同不確定性水平下，超越了所有基準，並且收益延伸至下游對齊。
2604.00997.author: Gyuseok Lee et.al.
2604.00997.authors: Gyuseok Lee, Wonbin Kweon, Zhenrui Yue, SeongKu Kang, Jiawei Han, Dong Wang
